title: Invasion of Poland (Fall Weiss)
summary: The German invasion of Poland on 1 September 1939 began the Second World War in Europe. Using combined-arms tactics — armour, motorised infantry, and air power — Germany defeated Poland in five weeks. A Soviet invasion from the east on 17 September eliminated any possibility of continued resistance.
outcome: German–Soviet victory. Poland partitioned between Germany and the Soviet Union under the secret protocol of the Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact. Warsaw surrendered 27 September; last organised resistance ended 6 October.

casualties.poland: ~66,000 killed, ~694,000 captured by Germany, ~230,000 by Soviets
